description: Add missing E2E test coverage for two documented, user-visible features: (1) popstate navigation hook and (2) log circular buffer overflow behavior.

userStories.title: Add popstate onNavigate lifecycle hook E2E test
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the onNavigate hook to fire on popstate events (browser back/forward), so that my plugin is notified of all in-page navigation types.
acceptanceCriteria: New test 'onNavigate fires on popstate (browser back)' exists in lifecycle-hooks.e2e.ts
Test triggers popstate by pushing a history entry then calling history.back()
Test asserts onNavigate recorded the original URL after going back
Test follows the same pattern as the existing hashchange test (waitFor polling, same timeout, same assertion style)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/lifecycle-hooks.e2e.ts

Add a new test inside the existing 'Lifecycle hooks' describe block (after the replaceState test at ~line 122).

Follow the exact pattern of the hashchange test (lines 62-88):
1. setupToolTest to get a page
2. Clear __opentabs_onNavigate_urls
3. Push a history entry with pushState to '/popstate-target'
4. Clear __opentabs_onNavigate_urls again (pushState itself triggers onNavigate)
5. Call history.back() — this fires popstate
6. waitFor polling until __opentabs_onNavigate_urls has entries (10s timeout, 200ms interval)
7. Assert the URL array contains the original URL (the test server URL without /popstate-target)

Key detail: history.back() is async — the popstate event fires after the current task completes. The waitFor polling handles this naturally since it retries until the URL appears.

The test plugin (plugins/e2e-test) already implements onNavigate by recording URLs to __opentabs_onNavigate_urls — no changes needed to the plugin.

userStories.title: Add log circular buffer overflow E2E test
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want an E2E test verifying the per-plugin log circular buffer enforces its 1000-entry limit, so that buffer overflow regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: New test 'log buffer enforces 1000-entry circular limit' exists in plugin-logging.e2e.ts
Test emits enough log entries to exceed the 1000-entry buffer limit
Test asserts logBufferSize from /health does not exceed 1000
Test asserts new entries are still recorded after the buffer is full (buffer wraps, doesn't stop)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/plugin-logging.e2e.ts

Add a new test after the existing tests (~line 131). Use the same fixture pattern as existing tests (mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient).

The e2e-test plugin has a 'log_levels' tool that emits 4 log entries per call (debug, info, warn, error). To exceed 1000 entries, call it ~260 times (260 * 4 = 1040 entries).

Strategy:
1. Setup tool test (setupToolTest)
2. Call log_levels tool in a loop ~260 times (use Promise.all in batches of 10-20 for speed, but serial is fine too since this is a slow test)
3. After all calls complete, poll /health until logBufferSize stabilizes
4. Assert logBufferSize <= 1000 (the documented limit)
5. Make one more log_levels call
6. Poll /health again
7. Assert logBufferSize is still <= 1000 (buffer wrapped, didn't grow)
8. Assert logBufferSize >= 1000 (buffer is at capacity, new entries replaced old ones)

Mark this test with test.slow() since it will take significant time to emit 1000+ log entries through the full dispatch pipeline.

The buffer limit (1000) is documented in platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md. The actual constant is LOG_BUFFER_MAX_SIZE in the server source — read platform/mcp-server/src/ to find the exact constant name and value to use in the test assertion.

Using opus because this test requires understanding the full log dispatch pipeline (tool → adapter → relay → background → WebSocket → server buffer) and careful batching strategy to avoid timeouts.
